title: benchmarks: push/pull
body:
Polish the push and pull benchmarks.  In particular, make sure to not be
network bound during these benchmarks by running a local registry and
pushing a local image that can later on be pulled.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <vrothberg@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes please follow the kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: I still can't test anything due to the `Timed out after 90.001s.` issue.
@edsantiago can you share where you get the timeout?

```
• Failure [104.078 seconds]                                                                                                                                     
Podman Benchmark Suite                                                                                                                                          
/home/esm/src/atomic/2018-02.podman/libpod/test/e2e/benchmarks_test.go:66                                                                                       
  Podman Benchmark Suite [Measurement]                                                                                                                          
  /home/esm/src/atomic/2018-02.podman/libpod/test/e2e/benchmarks_test.go:121                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                                
  Timed out after 90.001s.                                                                                                                                      
  Expected process to exit.  It did not.                                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                                
  /home/esm/src/atomic/2018-02.podman/libpod/test/utils/utils.go:368         
```

Very curious, thanks! Does it work with the following diff?
```diff
diff --git a/test/e2e/benchmarks_test.go b/test/e2e/benchmarks_test.go
index 0e7bc8a69c6c..0321190a1a68 100644
--- a/test/e2e/benchmarks_test.go
+++ b/test/e2e/benchmarks_test.go
@@ -181,7 +181,12 @@ var _ = Describe("Podman Benchmark Suite", func() {
                        session := podmanTest.Podman([]string{"push", "--tls-verify=false", "--creds", user + ":" + pass, UBI_INIT, "localhost:" + port + "/repo/image:tag"})
                        session.WaitWithDefaultTimeout()
                        Expect(session).Should(Exit(0))
-               }, &newBenchmarkOptions{needsRegistry: true})
+               }, &newBenchmarkOptions{
+                       needsRegistry: true,
+                       init: func() {
+                               podmanTest.AddImageToRWStore(UBI_INIT)
+                       },
+               })
 
                newBenchmark("podman pull", func() {
                        port, user, pass := getPortUserPass()
@@ -191,6 +196,7 @@ var _ = Describe("Podman Benchmark Suite", func() {
                }, &newBenchmarkOptions{
                        needsRegistry: true,
                        init: func() {
+                               podmanTest.AddImageToRWStore(UBI_INIT)
                                port, user, pass := getPortUserPass()
                                session := podmanTest.Podman([]string{"push", "--tls-verify=false", "--creds", user + ":" + pass, UBI_INIT, "localhost:" + port + "/repo/image:tag"})
                                session.WaitWithDefaultTimeout()
```

nope, looks like same error:
```
• Failure [109.763 seconds]
Podman Benchmark Suite
/home/esm/src/atomic/2018-02.podman/libpod/test/e2e/benchmarks_test.go:66
  Podman Benchmark Suite [Measurement]
  /home/esm/src/atomic/2018-02.podman/libpod/test/e2e/benchmarks_test.go:121

  Timed out after 90.001s.
  Expected process to exit.  It did not.

  /home/esm/src/atomic/2018-02.podman/libpod/test/utils/utils.go:368
```
Aaaah, I begin to believe it's the `podman-registry` script pulling.

@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL

Ed is out and I tested it locally disconnected from the network.
@flouthoc @Luap99 mind merging? I'll polish the e2e tests after.
